source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NOOKS.AI http://Nooks.ai:

Nooks is an applied AI lab building the Agent Workspace for GTM. We design AI agents that operate across the full sales action set, from account strategy to prospect research and outreach. Building 0→1 has never been easier, but selling at the speed of the market remains the last frontier for AI. Our mission is to 10x every seller by automating the busywork so they can focus on what humans do best: closing deals.



Today, over 1,500 customers run on Nooks, including Notion, HubSpot, Cursor, and Rippling. We’ve raised $70M+ from top VCs like Kleiner Perkins, which made its first sales-tech investment in over a decade with us. Since then, we’ve tripled ARR each year for 3 years running and grown a high-caliber team turning the art of sales into a scalable science.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Sales software was built for humans. But we believe most sales work will be done by agents within the next two years. We're building out multiple vertical products (AI Dialer, AI Prospecting, AI Sequencing, Agent Studio) to get there. Reps use Nooks as a "copilot" and spend 4+ hours a day across these different product lines. We're hiring a staff engineer to help set a unified technical direction for the entire team.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build AI features that drive revenue, end to end. Define the success metric, ship across React/TypeScript and Node.js/Python, and iterate until it moves. 1,100+ customers depend on this product daily.

 - Work directly with customers. Our biggest accounts have shared Slack channels with our engineers; you'll be on calls with users one day and shipping fixes back to them the next.

 - Set technical direction across the team. How should agent memory work per rep? What's the right primitive for a long-running outbound strategy? Make the architectural calls other engineers build against.

 - Raise the bar across the org. Mentor senior engineers, run the hardest design reviews, and help us hire more people like you.



YOU MIGHT BE A STRONG FIT IF:

 - 8+ years of engineering experience, including time as a tech lead of a meaningful product area, a founder, or a domain expert other engineers turn to.

 - Production experience with AI features. For example, you've debugged misbehaving agents and built the evals to keep issues from recurring.

 - Strong opinions, held loosely, about what the product surface of human-agent collaboration should look like.

 - Not afraid to hop on a call with a customer to validate a feature you just built.

 - You make the rest of the company faster.

 - Based in San Francisco, excited to be in-office 3+ days per week.



AT NOOKS YOU'LL ENJOY

 - A world-class team. Around 40 engineers, mostly former founders or founding engineers and veteran builders from places like Applied Intuition, Scale, Airtable, Citadel, and Jane Street. Thirteen Olympiad medals across the team.

 - A product still being figured out. We don't fully know what sales engagement looks like when agents do most of the work. The people who join now help decide.

 - Top-of-market compensation.






EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT

Nooks is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe in providing equal employment opportunities to all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Nooks does not discriminate in hiring, promotion, compensation, or any other employment practices, and we are committed to ensuring a workplace that is free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply and join our team.

Senior Technical Sourcer

 

The Role

 

Join Nooks as our Senior Technical Sourcer and play a critical role in building our exceptional team. You'll work closely with hiring managers to understand technical talent needs and design effective sourcing strategies. As a specialist in talent mapping and engagement, you'll identify and attract top technical professionals while ensuring a seamless and positive candidate experience throughout the recruitment process.

 

Responsibilities

 

 - Partner with hiring managers and team leads to understand talent requirements and develop compelling job descriptions for technical roles.

 - Develop and execute innovative sourcing strategies for a variety of technical positions across the organization.

 - Manage candidate pipelines, relationships, and communications to provide a best-in-class experience.

 - Track recruiting metrics and report on sourcing effectiveness and progress to hiring teams.

 - Maintain up-to-date knowledge of current market trends and talent pools in relevant tech areas.

 - Collaborate with recruiters and other stakeholders to support scalable recruiting programs and continuous process improvement.

 

Requirements

 

 - 3+ years of in-house technical sourcing experience at a high-growth tech startup or similar organization. Or have been in an executive recruiter/researcher role where you identified top talent.

 - Strong proficiency with sourcing tools, platforms.

 - Proficient in utilizing AI

 - Excellent relationship-building and communication skills, with a commitment to a great candidate experience.

 - Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage competing priorities.

 - Self-starter who thrives in dynamic, fast-paced environments.

 - San Francisco office attendance as required (typically 3 days/week).

We offer competitive compensation designed to attract and reward top talent. Our salary range for this role is $120,000 - $180,000, with additional equity, generous perks, and comprehensive benefits in accordance with pay transparency practices.

Since then, we’ve tripled ARR each year for 3 years running and grown a high-caliber team turning the art of sales into a scalable science.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Core Infrastructure team makes the rest of Nooks fast. We're going from one product to many — Sequencing, Dialer, Agent Studio, Coaching — and our data layer, observability, and developer tooling have to scale with that. The dialer alone runs ~100M calls a week, and reps spend 4+ hours a day in the product. We're hiring senior engineers to own the systems everything else is built on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build infrastructure that makes every product team faster. Data models, APIs, observability, CI/CD — pick the area, own it across the company.

 - Keep the rails on while we go multi-product. Capacity, reliability, cost. Set the standards other teams build against.

 - Make foundational technical decisions. What's the right shape for our data access layer when we're querying across calls, prospects, and accounts? How do we attribute infra cost to product teams cleanly?

 - Mentor others on the team. Run code and design reviews, and pair on the hardest problems.
   
   


YOU MIGHT BE A STRONG FIT IF:

 - 5+ years of engineering experience, with depth in distributed systems, databases, or developer infrastructure. We don't care which exact stack.

 - Production experience scaling systems through real growth (10x, 100x), and the scars that come with it.

 - Strong opinions, held loosely, about what good developer experience looks like inside a fast-moving product team.

 - Comfortable being the engineer everyone else calls during an incident.

 - You make the rest of the team faster.

 - Based in San Francisco, excited to be in-office 3+ days per week.
   
   


AT NOOKS YOU'LL ENJOY

 - A world-class team. Around 40 engineers, mostly former founders or founding engineers and veteran builders from places like Applied Intuition, Scale, Airtable, Citadel, and Jane Street. Thirteen Olympiad medals across the team.

 - A platform being built for the first time. Nooks was one product a year ago. The infra you build now decides what the next four products feel like.

 - Top-of-market compensation.

 - Salesfloor - sales reps & managers can work together throughout the day, listen to each others’ calls, give real-time advice, coaching, shadowing, onboarding, training.

ABOUT THE ROLE

Sales software was built for humans. But we believe most sales work will be done by agents within the next two years. Agent Studio is our bet to enable this: AI that does 90% of the non-calling, non-creative work an SDR team does today fully autonomously. We're piloting with a small set of design partners. As a Senior Software Engineer, you'll build those agents and iterate with customers until they see impact on their bottom line.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build AI agents that drive revenue, end to end. GM-level responsibility for your customers' revenue numbers. We're aiming to 5x their meetings booked within a 1 month trial.

 - Build agents across the breadth of a sales rep's workday. Account strategy, prospecting, outbound emails, LinkedIn DMs, gifting, call prep, followups. Each one requires going deep on how the rep actually works.

 - Fully automate nuanced work on the SDR's behalf. These agents should learn over time, calibrate trust with the user, and have clear evals that prove they work.

 - Work with customers. Our customers have shared Slack channels with our engineers; you'll be on calls with users one day and shipping agent improvements back to them the next.



YOU MIGHT BE A STRONG FIT IF:

 - 5+ years of engineering experience, ideally including time as a technical founder, founding engineer, or forward-deployed engineer at a place with a high bar.

 - Production experience with AI agents. For example, you've shipped a prompt or tool change in response to a specific user complaint that same week.

 - Strong opinions, held loosely, about which insights from the field belong in the platform vs as customer-facing modifiers.

 - High agency. Comfortable owning an outcome without a clear playbook.

 - Strong foundation in something close to our stack (React, TypeScript, Node.js, Python).

 - Based in San Francisco, excited to be in-office 3+ days per week.



AT NOOKS YOU'LL ENJOY

 - A world-class team. Around 40 engineers, mostly former founders or founding engineers and veteran builders from places like Applied Intuition, Scale, Airtable, Citadel, and Jane Street. Thirteen Olympiad medals across the team.

ABOUT THE ROLE



Nooks is the Agent Workspace for Sales, where reps and AI agents execute and decide next moves together across 1M+ interactions per day. As our second security engineer, you'll help secure a fast-growing, AI-native platform and the customer data that flows through it — across a rapidly expanding set of product surfaces. This is a foundational, high-ownership role: you'll shape our security program from the ground up, including the novel challenge of securing AI agents and how they're used within organizations.



What You'll Do

 - Own security reviews and threat modeling for new and existing product surfaces.

 - Build and improve core security processes (secure SDLC, detection & response).

 - Harden our cloud and application infrastructure and drive remediation of high-priority risks.

 - Help define how we secure AI agents and govern their use — guardrails, data exposure, prompt-injection and abuse risks.

 - Support enterprise customer trust: compliance (SOC 2), and customer-facing security needs.

 - Partner closely with product and infra engineering to embed security into how we ship.


YOU MIGHT BE A STRONG FIT IF:

 - 4 - 5+ years of hands-on information security experience.

 - Experience at a startup or early-stage company; comfort with ambiguity, autonomy, and high ownership.

 - Strong fundamentals in application and/or cloud security (AWS/GCP).

 - Pragmatic judgment — able to prioritize risk and do more with less.

 - Clear communication and a collaborative, low-ego style.

Nice to haves

 - Experience securing AI agents / LLM systems or governing AI agent use within an organization.

 - Enterprise compliance experience (SOC 2, ISO 27001).

At Nooks You'll Enjoy

 - An early seat on a small, high-leverage security team. You'll be one of our first security hires and help build out the function — its standards, tooling, and threat models — owning whole domains end-to-end as Nooks scales across many more product surfaces, with a clear path to grow into a senior or lead role.

 - A world-class team to build it with. Around 70 engineers, mostly former founders or founding engineers and veteran builders from places like Applied Intuition, Scale, Airtable, Citadel, and Jane Street, with thirteen Olympiad medals across the team. You'll partner with them to make security a first-class part of how we ship.

 - Frontier security problems. Securing AI agents and how they operate inside organizations is largely unsolved territory — you'll help define how it's done.

 - Top-of-market compensation.
